events.text: Hi Sage. I am here to run one prepared status test in Code Studio using the beginner instructions, and record the result in the note - the test name, the file and line for the failed check, what Playwright expected, and what the page actually returned. I am not changing any code, just reading the result.

events.outputSummary:
stty cols 101 rows 13
Welcome to the lab. This is a real shell — look around.
lab:/workspace$  stty cols 101 rows 13

lab:/workspace$ npm test


> test
> node scripts/test.mjs


Running 1 test using 
… [1019 chars truncated] …
or Context: test-results/status-garden-status-banner/error-context.md

  1 failed
    tests/status.spec.js:21:1 › garden status banner ───────────────────────────────────────────────
\lab:/workspace$

events.text: Honestly the big red "failed" line pulled my eye first and I nearly wrote that down as the result, but once I read further and found the paired Expected and Received lines it clicked - it is just the expected-vs-actual I already write by hand. Thanks Sage.
